workflow.critical: The workflow execution engine is governed by: ~/.nakiros/core/tasks/workflow.xml
You MUST have already loaded and processed: {installed_path}/workflow.yaml
YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in the configured communication_language resolved from profile and workspace settings.
Generate technical agent artifacts (challenge.md, implementation notes, validation) in ENGLISH for agent performance.
Generate all PM-facing content (ticket comments, review package) in {{document_language}}.
Commits are ALWAYS in ENGLISH (dev standard). This is non-negotiable.
Execute ALL steps in exact order; do NOT skip steps.
Do not report "completed" until all implementation, validation, and PM sync activities are truly finished.

step.goal: Load and validate nakiros configuration (silent mode)
step.action: Run silently: `nakiros workspace get` (auto-detected from cwd). Parse the JSON output and store as {{workspace_get_result}}. The field {{workspace_get_result.artifacts}} contains the localPath of every available context file. If the command fails or nakiros is not installed, continue without remote context (degraded mode).
Run silently in background: `nakiros sync start`. This starts the file watcher that will push any context file modified during this session to the cloud. Ignore failures silently.
Load profile config from {profile_config} when available (optional).

Apply defaults when missing: user_name={default_user_name}, idle_threshold_minutes={default_idle_threshold_minutes}, communication_language={default_communication_language}, document_language={default_document_language}.

Resolve workspace scope from {workspace_config}. If the repo pointer exists, read `workspace_slug` from `{project-root}/_nakiros/workspace.yaml`, then load `~/.nakiros/workspaces/{workspace_slug}/workspace.yaml` as the canonical workspace map. If the pointer is missing, continue in single-repo mode rooted at {project-root}.

Load workspace-global context first by calling context.workspace.get for keys product, global, interRepo, and architecture when available. Use repo-local `_nakiros/` slices only after the workspace-level map is understood.
Set {{workspace_repo_list}} from canonical workspace yaml `repos`. In single-repo fallback mode, set it to the current repo only.
Set {{pm_tool}} from canonical workspace yaml `workspace.pm_tool`, defaulting to "none" when absent.
Set {{branch_pattern}} from canonical workspace yaml `workspace.branch_pattern` when present, fallback to {profile_config} branch_pattern.
Override {{document_language}} with `workspace.document_language`.
Infer {{git_host}} from the primary repo URL in {{workspace_repo_list}} using the provider domain or SSH host (github, gitlab, gitea). Leave empty if it cannot be determined.

Resolve {{repo_slug}} from the primary impacted repo in {{workspace_repo_list}}. If only one repo exists, use it. If multiple repos are impacted and no primary repo is clear, ask the user which repo owns this execution before proceeding.
Set gitflow defaults when workspace settings omit them: feature={default_feature_base_branch}, bugfix={default_bugfix_base_branch}, hotfix={default_hotfix_base_branch}.
Validate required delivery settings: pm_tool, git_host, branch_pattern.

action.if: pm_tool is none or git_host is empty or branch_pattern is empty

step.action: HALT: "✗ Blocking workspace configuration error: missing pmTool, branchPattern, or a detectable git host. Complete workspace settings in Nakiros Desktop before starting dev-story."
step.output: [WORKFLOW] Étape 1/9 — Load configuration ✓ Config loaded and validated

step.action: Read {active_run_file} if present.

check.if: an active run exists and is not completed
check.ask:
Do you want to resume this run or close it before starting another one?
---
**What would you like to do?**
1. Resume this run 2. Close it and start a new one

check.action: Resume existing run and continue.
Mark previous run closed, then continue.

step.goal: Resolve ticket context and transition to In Progress
step.action: If a ticket ID is provided, retrieve the ticket via the configured Nakiros PM integration before challenge/implementation.
If pm_tool is configured and no ticket provided, ask user to choose: work on existing ticket or create new ticket.

action.if: pm_tool is not none and ticket was retrieved

step.action: Capture PM tracking identifiers (ticket key/id, workspace/project identifiers, available transitions) needed for status, comments, and worklog operations.

check.ask:
Ticket retrieval failed. Retry via the configured PM integration or continue in standalone mode? (retry/standalone)
---
**What would you like to do?**
1. Retry retrieval 2. Continue in standalone mode

check.action: Retry retrieval once and continue if successful.
Continue with explicit ambiguity warning and queue sync retry metadata in {sync_queue_file}.

action.if: pm_tool is not none and ticket was retrieved

step.action: IMMEDIATELY transition ticket to {pm_in_progress_status} via the Nakiros PM action/integration layer with comment: "Starting analysis and implementation preparation. Run ID: {{runId}}"

action.if: pm_tool is not none and ticket was retrieved

step.action: Capture timestamp as {{worklog_start_time}} for worklog calculation (includes challenge + implementation duration).

check.action: Log warning: "⚠ Could not transition ticket to In Progress. Queued in {sync_queue_file} for retry. Continuing with implementation."

step.output: [WORKFLOW] Étape 3/9 — Resolve ticket context ✓ Ticket context resolved | PM status: In Progress | Worklog tracking started

step.critical: No implementation starts before challenge is thoroughly passed. This is a critical gate.
step.action: Capture current active persona as {{entry_persona}}.
Load PM persona from ~/.nakiros/agents/pm.md for this challenge gate.

Conduct rigorous PM-quality challenge on the ticket analyzing: 1. CLARITY: Is the user story or ticket description clear and unambiguous? 2. CONSISTENCY: Do acceptance criteria align with ticket intent? Any contradictions? 3. SCOPE: Is the scope bounded and realistic? Any hidden dependencies? 4. ALIGNMENT: Does this ticket make sense in the project context? Check against project goals/architecture if available. 5. TESTABILITY: Are acceptance criteria measurable and testable? Can QA/user verify completion? 6. RISKS: What could go wrong? What dependencies exist? What's the impact of this work?

Before concluding the challenge, reconcile the ticket against workspace-global product and architecture context, then use repo-local `_nakiros/` detail only for implementation ownership and constraints.

Load architect-defined technical execution tasks when available from the story, linked backlog tasks, or `_nakiros/backlog/tasks/`. Treat those tasks as the execution source of truth for sequencing when they exist.

Create challenge artifact at {workflow_steps_dir}/{{runId}}-challenge.md (ENGLISH) with these sections: - **Ticket Intent**: What problem does this solve? - **Acceptance Criteria** (checked): ✓ or ✗ for each AC - **PM Challenge Verdict**: PASS or FAIL with justification - **Scope Boundaries**: What's included, what's out-of-scope - **Implementation Approach**: High-level technical direction - **Technical Task Plan**: ordered list of architect-defined tasks to execute, or an explicit note that no task breakdown was found - **Risks & Blockers**: What could go wrong? Any known issues? - **Dependencies**: Other tickets, external systems, prerequisites - **Testability Notes**: How to verify implementation is complete

check.if: PM challenge identifies critical gaps or ambiguities
check.action: Restore persona to {{entry_persona}}.
Set run state to awaiting_user.
check.ask:
⚠️ Challenge gate FAILED. Issues found: [List the specific gaps/ambiguities]
Please clarify the ticket or update it in the configured PM tool before implementation can proceed. Waiting for your input.

check.if: ticket is clear and challenge passes

action.if: pm_tool is not none and ticket was retrieved

check.action: Post challenge summary comment on ticket in {{document_language}}: "✓ Challenge Gate PASSED - Scope: [1-2 sentences on what will be built] - Approach: [1-2 sentences on technical direction] - Key Risks: [bullet points if any] - Branch planning: pending gitflow resolution | Run: {{runId}}"
Restore persona to {{entry_persona}} before step 5+ implementation work.
check.output: [WORKFLOW] Étape 4/9 — PM challenge gate ✓ Gate PASSED — ready for implementation

step.goal: Resolve gitflow branch, create dedicated worktree, and persist run state
step.action: Classify work type before touching git: - hotfix if the ticket is explicitly a production incident, urgent production fix, or tagged hotfix - bugfix if it is a defect but not production-critical - feature otherwise
Resolve branch name from ticket ID + slug using branch_pattern from effective config, while preserving the gitflow family (`feature/`, `bugfix/`, `hotfix/`, or workspace equivalent).
Resolve {{worktree_path}} under {worktrees_root_dir}/{{repo_slug}}/{{branchName}}.
Create or reuse a dedicated git worktree at {{worktree_path}} bound to {{branchName}} from {{branch_base}}. Never execute implementation on the repo's default checkout.
Confirm the worktree is clean before implementation starts. If not clean, HALT with a blocking message naming the conflicting files or branch state.
Ensure directories exist: {workflow_runs_dir} and {workflow_steps_dir}.

Persist active run state to {active_run_file} and run artifact to {workflow_runs_dir}/{{runId}}.json, including: - ticketId, branchName, branch_base, worktree_path, runId - status: "in_progress" - timestamps: challenge_started_at, implementation_started_at, last_activity_at - worklog_start_time: {{worklog_start_time}} (for final worklog calculation) - pm_tool, git_host, document_language (for later reporting)
step.output: [WORKFLOW] Étape 5/9 — Gitflow and worktree ✓ Branch resolved: {{branchName}} from {{branch_base}} | Worktree: {{worktree_path}} | Run ID: {{runId}}

step.goal: Implement scope with test-first discipline + mandatory commit
step.critical: Follow the approved challenge scope EXACTLY; do not implement out-of-scope features.
At the end of this step, you MUST make a git commit with conventional commit messages.
step.action: Load Developer persona from ~/.nakiros/agents/dev.md for implementation and test execution.
Plan concrete implementation work from the challenge + workspace-global context + ticket context + architect-defined technical tasks.
If architect-defined technical tasks exist, respect their sequencing as authoritative. Execute them in order unless the task metadata explicitly allows parallelization.
If the story lacks a usable technical task breakdown and implementation order is unclear, HALT and ask for Architect clarification instead of inventing a new breakdown silently.
When multiple tasks exist, track which task is active (`back`, `front`, `tests`, etc.) and update run notes as the workflow advances through them.
For multi-repo stories, treat workspace-global architecture as the dependency map and execute repo-local work from the dedicated owning repo worktree only.
Write failing tests first when feasible (red), implement minimal code (green), then refactor safely (refactor).
Run targeted and regression tests.
Update run artifacts and notes under {workspace_root_dir} as work progresses, always from inside {{worktree_path}}.

action.if: pm_tool is not none and ticket was retrieved

step.action: Publish at least one progress comment during implementation through the configured PM integration with done/next/blockers. Format: "✓ Done: [X] | Next: [Y] | Blockers: [Z]" and update last_activity_at in run state.

check.if: implementation is blocked and pm_tool is not none and ticket was retrieved
check.action: Transition ticket to {pm_blocked_status} via the Nakiros PM action/integration layer (if available), otherwise add explicit blocker comment.

action.if: required dependency or architectural change exceeds scope

step.action: ASK user approval: "This change exceeds approved scope. Approve to proceed? (yes/no)"

After all implementation is complete: 1. Run all tests one final time (regression check). 2. Generate conventional commit messages based on changes: - Use "feat:" for new features (from ticket intent) - Use "fix:" for bug fixes - Use "refactor:" for code refactoring - Use "test:" for test-only changes - Add ticket ID in footer: "Fixes {{ticketId}}" 3. Stage all changes: git add . 4. Commit with message(s): "feat: [brief description from ticket] ({{ticketId}})
- Implementation detail 1 - Implementation detail 2 - Testing notes
Fixes {{ticketId}}"
Push branch to remote immediately after commit from {{worktree_path}}: git push -u origin {{branchName}}
Update run state: mark implementation_completed_at timestamp.
step.output: [WORKFLOW] Étape 6/9 — Implementation ✓ Implementation complete | Changes committed to {{branchName}}

step.goal: Generate review package and request developer validation
step.critical: Review package is MANDATORY. Create file, display to developer, and get explicit approval before proceeding.
step.action: STEP 7.1: Analyze all changes made since branch creation. 1. Run: git diff {{branch_base}}..HEAD 2. Identify all modified files 3. Note test additions/modifications 4. Understand the overall implementation scope

STEP 7.2: Generate comprehensive review package content in {{document_language}}. Use git diff analysis to fill in concrete details for each section:
**Title**: [Brief summary of what was implemented, extracted from commit message and challenge scope]
**Description**: [1-2 paragraphs: What problem does this solve? What was implemented? Why this approach?]
**Technical Choices**: - [Key decision 1 with justification] - [Key decision 2 with justification] - [Libraries/tools introduced and why]
**Technical Task Execution Order**: - [Task 1]: [Why first] - [Task 2]: [Why next]
**Changes Made** (from git diff): - [File 1]: [What changed and why] - [File 2]: [What changed and why] [List ALL significant files changed]
**Testing**: - Unit tests: [List files and what they test] - Integration tests: [If applicable] - Manual testing: [Step-by-step verification instructions]
**How to Run**: 1. [Setup prerequisites] 2. [Build command] 3. [Run command to demonstrate feature] 4. [Expected output/behavior]
**Deployment Notes**: - Migrations: [Yes/No - describe if yes] - Environment variables: [List if any] - Breaking changes: [Yes/No - describe if yes] - Rollback plan: [How to rollback if needed]
**Reviewed By**: [Developer name] **Reviewed At**: [Current timestamp]
STEP 7.3: WRITE the review package file to {workflow_runs_dir}/{{runId}}-mr-report.md with the generated content.
STEP 7.4: Display the review package to the developer and ask for explicit validation.
step.ask:
✓ Review package generated and saved to: {workflow_runs_dir}/{{runId}}-mr-report.md
Please review carefully: - [ ] Title and description accurately reflect the work? - [ ] All files changed are documented? - [ ] Testing instructions are clear and complete? - [ ] Deployment notes are accurate?
---
**What would you like to do?**
1. Approve the review package 2. Edit specific sections 3. Regenerate with new guidance

check.action: Ask developer what needs to be changed in the report.
Apply the requested edits to the review package file.
Display updated report and re-ask for validation.
Ask developer for guidance on what should be different.
Re-generate the review package with new guidance and save to file.
Display updated report and re-ask for validation.
STEP 7.5: Generate SCM review action payload at {workflow_runs_dir}/{{runId}}-mr-payload.json with source branch, target branch, title, and description extracted from the approved review package.
Mark run state: mr_report_approved_at = now, mr_report_path = {workflow_runs_dir}/{{runId}}-mr-report.md, mr_payload_path = {workflow_runs_dir}/{{runId}}-mr-payload.json
check.output: [WORKFLOW] Étape 7/9 — Review package ✓ Review package approved and SCM review payload prepared

step.action: Load and execute validation checklist from {validation}.
If checklist fails, return to implementation/validation until pass or HALT with explicit blockers.

step.goal: Move to Review, prepare MR action handoff, sync ticket, and finalize worklog
step.critical: This is the final step. Ensure all syncs are complete before marking done.
step.action: Restore persona to {{entry_persona}} before final workflow status communication.

check.if: work is complete and validation passed and MR report approved

action.if: pm_tool is not none and ticket was retrieved

check.action: Compute time spent from {{worklog_start_time}} to now, rounding to {worklog_rounding_minutes} minutes with minimum {minimum_worklog_minutes} minutes. If timestamps unavailable, ask user: "How much time did you spend on this ticket (in minutes)?" Store as {{total_worklog_minutes}}.
STEP 9.2a: Do NOT create the MR/PR directly in this workflow. Treat {workflow_runs_dir}/{{runId}}-mr-payload.json as the canonical handoff artifact for a future Nakiros SCM action.
STEP 9.2b: If the user already created the MR/PR manually, capture the provided URL as {{mr_url}}. Otherwise leave {{mr_url}} empty and keep the payload ready for later backend execution.

action.if: pm_tool is not none and ticket was retrieved

check.action: Transition ticket to {pm_review_status} (or {pm_done_status} if no review stage) via the Nakiros PM action/integration layer.

action.if: pm_tool is not none and ticket was retrieved

check.action:
Add final completion comment to ticket in {{document_language}}: "✓ IMPLEMENTATION COMPLETE
Branch: {{branchName}} Worktree: {{worktree_path}} Review package: {workflow_runs_dir}/{{runId}}-mr-report.md MR action payload: {workflow_runs_dir}/{{runId}}-mr-payload.json MR: {{mr_url}} Time spent: {{total_worklog_minutes}} minutes
Delivered: - All acceptance criteria met - Architect-defined technical tasks executed in approved order - Tests passing (unit + regression) - Code review ready
Next: Await code review and MR/PR creation via Nakiros action or manual submission"

action.if: pm_tool is not none and ticket was retrieved

check.action: Push worklog via the configured PM integration: {{total_worklog_minutes}} minutes
Log warning: "⚠ Final PM/MR sync failed. Queued in {sync_queue_file} for retry."
Queue retry metadata with MR URL, worklog, and status transition details.
Run silently: `nakiros sync push` (flush final state of all context files to cloud). Ignore failures silently.
Run silently: `nakiros sync stop` (stops the background file watcher). Ignore failures silently.
Mark run state as completed and persist to {active_run_file} and {workflow_runs_dir}/{{runId}}.json.
Update run state: completed_at = now, status = "completed", final_mr_url = {{mr_url}}, final_worktree_path = {{worktree_path}}
check.output: [WORKFLOW] ✅ COMPLETE ├─ Ticket: {{ticketId}} → Review ├─ Branch: {{branchName}} (base: {{branch_base}}) ├─ Worktree: {{worktree_path}} ├─ Review package: {workflow_runs_dir}/{{runId}}-mr-report.md ├─ MR payload: {workflow_runs_dir}/{{runId}}-mr-payload.json ├─ MR: {{mr_url}} ├─ Time: {{total_worklog_minutes}} minutes └─ Artifacts: {workflow_runs_dir}/{{runId}}/

check.action: Restore run state to in_progress.
check.output: [WORKFLOW] ✗ DoD validation failed. Return to implementation.

action.if: pm_tool is not none and ticket was retrieved

check.action: Add ticket comment: "Awaiting clarification on [topic]. Paused implementation."
Mark run state as awaiting_user and persist atomically.
